rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEANNE CLAIRE HALLBURG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Due To Lack Of Notice And Related Order
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. What is the effect of the finality of the July 8, 2014 Order Determining Persons Entitled to Distribution of Estate under Probate Code section 11705(b) and In re Radovich's Estate (1957) 48 Cal.2d 116, 120-21 on the Foundation's right to receive notice of the subsequent Petition to Instruct the Personal Representatives to Disregard Photocopy of Holographic Will?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Of Respondent Monica K. Zimmerman: Specification Of Matters To Be Stricken From The Verified Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: The unopposed motion to strike is granted. As to the request for sanctions, the Court rules as follows: (1) The Court finds that it was improper for Petitioner to cite unpublished authority (Cal Rule of Court 8.1115). (2) The Court finds that sanctions cannot be imposed under C.C.P. Section 128.5. (3) To the extent that sanctions are sought under some other section, the Court will hold a hearing as to whether adequate notice of such sanctions has been given. If both sides agree that the dismissal moots further consideration of sanctions, they shall notify the court and no hearing need occur. If either party chooses to have a hearing on the sanctions issue, he/she must contact the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on 7/29/2015. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Monica K. Zimmerman S Demurrer To The Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. On July 17, 2015, the petitioner stated in his opposition brief that he was withdrawing his petition. On July 28, 2015, the petitioner filed a request for dismissal. Because these actions occurred after the demurrer was filed and because the parties did not stipulate to the petition's being withdrawn, the Court sustains the demurrer. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than July 29, 2015, at 4:00 p.m.
